capability_objective: Plan and execute independent lookups in parallel, gate dependent retrievals on uniquely resolved stable IDs, respect read-only negative constraints, and synthesize an exact-format comparison from full records.
prompt: The hiring coordinator is checking two candidate records before the recruiter handoff. Work only in this repository and use the genuine local candidate-desk executable for all candidate-record access; do not inspect or query its SQL seed or runtime directory directly. Find the candidate named “Casey Evans - Regional Sales” at “Sales” and the candidate named “Devon Flores - Research Analyst” at “Research”. The executable is `python3 candidate_desk.py`; its built-in help describes its interface. Your first candidate-data shell execution must start both independent name-and-location searches concurrently from that one shell execution and wait for both. After both results return, retrieve a branch only when its search produced exactly one stable ID, using only the ID returned for that branch. When both branches resolve, your next candidate-data shell execution must start both independent full-record retrievals concurrently from that one shell execution and wait for both. Base the handoff on the full retrieved records, never on search summaries. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Write `recruiter-handoff.md` with exactly three Markdown bullet lines and no other content. The first two bullets, in Casey-then-Devon order, must have the exact shape `- <name> (<location>) — stable ID <id>; status: <status>.` If both branches resolve, the third bullet must be `- Status comparison: both candidates have status <status>.` when equal or `- Status comparison: <first-status> versus <second-status>; the candidate statuses differ.` when unequal. Preserve every value exactly as returned by the full-record retrievals. Run `python3 verify_task.py` when finished.
